---
title: "Phase 1 — pointing selection"
subtitle: "Five HLWAS F158 re-observed pairs spanning the survey footprint"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pathlib import Path
import warnings
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from astropy.coordinates import SkyCoord, get_sun
from astropy.coordinates.errors import NonRotationTransformationWarning
from astropy.table import Table
from astropy.time import Time
import astropy.units as u

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=NonRotationTransformationWarning)

REPO = Path("../..").resolve()
# PAIRS: one row per PAIR_ID (5 rows). POINTINGS: one row per dithered
# exposure (30 rows = 5 pairs × 2 visits × 3 dithers).
PAIRS = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/detection/selected_pairs.ecsv",
                   format="ascii.ecsv").to_pandas()
POINTINGS = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/detection/selected_pointings.ecsv",
                       format="ascii.ecsv").to_pandas()
YEAR = int(PAIRS["SIM_DATE"].iloc[0][:4])
ELONG_MIN, ELONG_MAX = 90.0, 115.0
```

## Input and filters

The candidate pool is `catalogs/HLWAS.sim.ecsv` — the simulated Roman
High-Latitude Wide-Area Survey pointing list. We apply these filters to
arrive at the selection pool:

1. `BANDPASS == "F158"`.
2. Exactly two visits share identical (RA, Dec, PA) at 10⁻⁵ deg
   precision — these are the survey-design re-observations. Visit
   centres are computed with a **circular mean over the 3 dither RAs**
   (important near RA = 0°/360°: a linear mean would place a dither
   triplet straddling the wrap at a phantom RA ≈ 120°/240°).
3. The two visits are from `PASS = 15` and `PASS = 16` — the dominant
   revisit mode in HLWAS.
4. `MA_TABLE_NUMBER == 1007` (the 107.52 s F158 imaging cadence; the
   only MA table used by the (15,16) re-observations).
5. **Main-survey box:** `RA ∈ [330°, 360°] ∪ [0°, 50°]` and
   `Dec ∈ [−25°, −10°]`. The HLWAS F158 footprint is a single connected
   strip running through RA = 0° with hard RA and Dec edges; the box
   is chosen to sit well inside that strip.
6. **Interior check:** at least 200 F158 exposures sit within 2° of the
   pair centre. This drops the remaining near-edge pairs (e.g. along
   the southern Dec = −25° cut).

After filtering, every visit has exactly 3 dithered exposures, PA is
uniform within a pair (both visits share a roll), and the nominal RA/Dec
centres are pairwise identical. Each pair therefore gives **6 dithered
exposures at one (RA, Dec, PA) with two different observation dates**.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Funnel from the full HLWAS table down to the candidate pool for selection."
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO / "scripts/detection"))
from importlib import import_module
_mod = import_module("01_select_pointings")  # reuse circular-mean helper

RA_MIN_A, RA_MAX_B = _mod.RA_MIN_A, _mod.RA_MAX_B
DEC_MIN, DEC_MAX = _mod.DEC_MIN, _mod.DEC_MAX
INTERIOR_MIN, INTERIOR_R = _mod.INTERIOR_MIN, _mod.INTERIOR_RADIUS_DEG

t = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/HLWAS.sim.ecsv", format="ascii.ecsv")
f158 = t[t["BANDPASS"] == "F158"].to_pandas()
vkey = ["PLAN", "PASS", "SEGMENT", "OBSERVATION", "VISIT"]
vg = f158.groupby(vkey)
# Wrap-aware visit centres
centres_raw = vg["DEC"].mean().reset_index(name="DEC")
centres_raw["RA"] = vg["RA"].apply(
    lambda s: _mod._circular_mean_deg(s.to_numpy())).values
centres_raw["ra_r"] = centres_raw["RA"].round(5)
centres_raw["dec_r"] = centres_raw["DEC"].round(5)
pos_sizes = centres_raw.groupby(["ra_r", "dec_r"]).size()
pair_idx = pos_sizes[pos_sizes == 2].index
pair_pass = (centres_raw.set_index(["ra_r", "dec_r"]).loc[pair_idx]
             .reset_index().groupby(["ra_r", "dec_r"])["PASS"]
             .apply(lambda s: tuple(sorted(s.tolist()))))
n_1516 = int((pair_pass == (15, 16)).sum())

pairs_1516 = centres_raw.set_index(["ra_r", "dec_r"]).loc[
    pair_pass[pair_pass == (15, 16)].index].reset_index()
# MA table filter (requires merge with raw metadata)
vfirst = vg[["MA_TABLE_NUMBER"]].first().reset_index()
pairs_1516 = pairs_1516.merge(vfirst, on=vkey)
pairs_ma = pairs_1516[pairs_1516["MA_TABLE_NUMBER"] == 1007]
n_ma = int((pairs_ma.groupby(["ra_r", "dec_r"]).size() == 2).sum())

# Main-survey box
in_ra = (pairs_ma["RA"] >= RA_MIN_A) | (pairs_ma["RA"] <= RA_MAX_B)
in_dec = (pairs_ma["DEC"] >= DEC_MIN) & (pairs_ma["DEC"] <= DEC_MAX)
pairs_box = pairs_ma[in_ra & in_dec]
pairs_box = pairs_box.groupby(["ra_r", "dec_r"]).filter(lambda g: len(g) == 2)
n_box = pairs_box.groupby(["ra_r", "dec_r"]).ngroups

# Load the final result to report the interior-filtered count
n_final = Table.read(
    REPO / "catalogs/detection/selected_pairs.ecsv",
    format="ascii.ecsv").to_pandas().shape[0]  # = N selected

n_interior = len(PAIRS)  # 5 selected — but we want the pool size
# Reconstruct the pool size by calling the script helper:
pairs_pool, _ = _mod.load_pair_candidates()
pairs_pool = _mod.apply_interior_filter(pairs_pool, f158)
n_interior_pool = pairs_pool["PAIR_ID"].nunique()

funnel = pd.DataFrame([
    ("HLWAS.sim.ecsv — all rows",                        len(t)),
    ("F158 rows",                                         len(f158)),
    ("F158 visits (3-dither groups)",                    len(vg)),
    ("positions with exactly 2 visits",                  len(pair_idx)),
    ("… of which PASS pair = (15, 16)",                  n_1516),
    ("… and MA_TABLE_NUMBER = 1007",                     n_ma),
    (f"… inside RA ∈ [{RA_MIN_A:.0f}°, 360°] ∪ [0°, "
     f"{RA_MAX_B:.0f}°], Dec ∈ [{DEC_MIN:+.0f}°, {DEC_MAX:+.0f}°]",   n_box),
    (f"… interior (≥ {INTERIOR_MIN} F158 exps within "
     f"{INTERIOR_R:.0f}° of centre)",                    n_interior_pool),
    (f"… selected ({n_final} picks)",                    n_final),
], columns=["filter step", "count"])
funnel
```

## Solar-elongation window

Each selected pair is simulated on a date where the solar elongation of
the field is in **[90°, 115°]**, as close to 90° as possible — minimum
elongation drives maximum zodiacal brightness, which makes the recovery
test conservative (hardest detection), while staying inside Roman's
viewable-zone constraint. The chosen date minimises `(elong − 90°)`
while staying inside the window.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Solar elongation vs day of year 2026 for the five selected pairs. Grey band = [90°, 115°]; filled dot = the chosen simulation date (closest to 90° inside the window). Each pair has two windows per year; we pick the first-hit day for reproducibility."
start = Time(f"{YEAR}-01-01T00:00:00", scale="utc")
doys = np.arange(0, 366)
times = start + doys * u.day
sun = get_sun(times)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
cmap = plt.get_cmap("tab10")
for i, prow in PAIRS.iterrows():
    tgt = SkyCoord(ra=prow["RA"] * u.deg, dec=prow["DEC"] * u.deg)
    elong = sun.separation(tgt).deg
    ax.plot(doys, elong, color=cmap(i % 10),
            label=f"pair {int(prow['PAIR_ID'])} "
                  f"(RA={prow['RA']:.1f}°, Dec={prow['DEC']:.1f}°, "
                  f"ecl_lat={prow['ECL_LAT_DEG']:+.1f}°)")
    if prow["SIM_DATE"]:
        d = (Time(prow["SIM_DATE"]) - start).jd
        ax.plot(d, prow["SOLAR_ELONG_DEG"], "o", color=cmap(i % 10),
                markersize=10, markeredgecolor="k")
ax.axhspan(ELONG_MIN, ELONG_MAX, color="k", alpha=0.08,
           label=f"[{ELONG_MIN:.0f}°, {ELONG_MAX:.0f}°]")
ax.axhline(ELONG_MIN, color="k", ls="--", lw=0.7)
ax.axhline(ELONG_MAX, color="k", ls="--", lw=0.7)
ax.set_xlabel(f"Day of year {YEAR}")
ax.set_ylabel("Solar elongation (deg)")
ax.set_ylim(0, 180)
ax.legend(fontsize=8, loc="upper right")
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.show()
```

## The selection

Five pairs were chosen by binning the interior-filtered pool into 5
equal-count bins along the (wrap-aware) RA axis of the selection box
and picking the median-Dec pair in each bin.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "The five selected pairs. SIM_DATE is the chosen simulation date for both visits in the pair. WINDOWS_DOY lists all 2026 day-of-year ranges where the field's solar elongation is inside [90°, 115°]."
PAIRS[["PAIR_ID", "RA", "DEC", "PA", "ECL_LON_DEG", "ECL_LAT_DEG",
        "SIM_DATE", "SOLAR_ELONG_DEG", "WINDOWS_DOY"]].style.format(
    {"RA": "{:.3f}", "DEC": "{:.3f}", "PA": "{:.1f}",
     "ECL_LON_DEG": "{:.2f}", "ECL_LAT_DEG": "{:+.2f}",
     "SOLAR_ELONG_DEG": "{:.2f}"})
```

The set spans the main-survey RA box from 332.5° across the wrap to
42.1°, with ecliptic latitudes from about −32° to −6°. Within a tight
Dec ≈ −17° slice, this gives a mild range of zodiacal conditions while
keeping the field well inside the HLWAS footprint at every pick.

## Sky distribution

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "HLWAS F158 exposures (grey cloud), (15,16) interior pairs that pass all filters (blue), and the five selected pairs (red stars, annotated by PAIR_ID). Dashed rectangle = the main-survey selection box. The HLWAS F158 footprint is a connected strip through RA = 0° with hard edges; the box sits well inside it."
pool_centres = pairs_pool.groupby("PAIR_ID")[["RA", "DEC"]].first()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 6))
ax.scatter(f158["RA"], f158["DEC"], s=0.5, color="lightgray", alpha=0.5,
           label=f"{len(f158):,} HLWAS F158 exposures (all sky)")
ax.scatter(pool_centres["RA"], pool_centres["DEC"], s=6, color="tab:blue",
           alpha=0.5,
           label=f"{len(pool_centres)} (15,16) interior pairs in box")
for _, prow in PAIRS.iterrows():
    ax.scatter(prow["RA"], prow["DEC"], s=140, marker="*",
               color="tab:red", edgecolor="k", lw=0.6, zorder=5)
    ax.annotate(f" {int(prow['PAIR_ID'])}",
                (prow["RA"], prow["DEC"]), fontsize=10)
for ra_lo, ra_hi in [(RA_MIN_A, 360.0), (0.0, RA_MAX_B)]:
    ax.plot([ra_lo, ra_hi, ra_hi, ra_lo, ra_lo],
            [DEC_MIN, DEC_MIN, DEC_MAX, DEC_MAX, DEC_MIN],
            color="k", ls="--", lw=1.0, alpha=0.6)
ax.set_xlabel("RA (deg)")
ax.set_ylabel("Dec (deg)")
ax.set_xlim(360, 0)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
ax.legend(loc="lower right", markerscale=2)
plt.show()
```

## The pointing-level file

`catalogs/detection/selected_pointings.ecsv` is the artefact Phase 2
consumes. It has one row per *dithered exposure* — 5 pairs × 2 visits ×
3 dithers = 30 rows.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "The 30 selected pointings. RA/DEC here are the per-dither positions — not the nominal pair centre — and will be handed directly to `romanisim-make-image`."
POINTINGS[["PAIR_ID", "PASS", "SEGMENT", "OBSERVATION", "VISIT", "EXPOSURE",
           "RA", "DEC", "PA", "SIM_DATE", "SOLAR_ELONG_DEG"]]
```

## Self-checks

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "End-of-phase assertions on the selection output."
visits = POINTINGS.drop_duplicates(
    ["PAIR_ID", "PASS", "SEGMENT", "OBSERVATION", "VISIT"])
checks = [
    ("5 pairs selected",                       len(PAIRS) == 5),
    ("10 visits (2 per pair)",                 len(visits) == 10),
    ("30 pointings (3 dithers per visit)",     len(POINTINGS) == 30),
    ("all pair centres in Dec box",
     bool(PAIRS["DEC"].between(DEC_MIN, DEC_MAX).all())),
    ("all pair centres in RA box",
     bool(((PAIRS["RA"] >= RA_MIN_A) | (PAIRS["RA"] <= RA_MAX_B)).all())),
    ("all MA_TABLE_NUMBER = 1007",
     bool((POINTINGS["MA_TABLE_NUMBER"] == 1007).all())),
    ("all elongations in [90, 115]",
     bool(PAIRS["SOLAR_ELONG_DEG"].between(ELONG_MIN, ELONG_MAX).all())),
    ("pair centres pairwise distinct",
     PAIRS[["RA", "DEC"]].round(3).drop_duplicates().shape[0] == 5),
    ("both visits of a pair share (RA,Dec,PA) at nominal centres",
     bool(visits.groupby("PAIR_ID")[["RA", "DEC", "PA"]]
            .nunique().eq(1).all(axis=None))),
]
pd.DataFrame(checks, columns=["check", "pass"])
```

## Reproducing

```bash
pixi run python scripts/detection/01_select_pointings.py
```

Writes `catalogs/detection/selected_pairs.ecsv` (pair-level, 10 rows),
`catalogs/detection/selected_pointings.ecsv` (per-dither, 30 rows — the
Phase 2 input), and the two diagnostic PNGs under
`output/detection/phase1/`.

## Checkpoint

Phase 1 deliverable — ready for user review before starting Phase 2
(skycell / SCA overlap analysis, no new simulations).
